column8: Today he (tell) you if he (play) chess with you on Saturday evening.
column9: We (be) not sure if this friendship (last) long.
column10: I wonder when he (return) my finest collection of records.
column11: Many people believe that the time (come) when there (be) a universal parliament of the world.
column12: The poor fellow (wonder) if the next mail (bring) better news from home.
column13: I (not know) if the local people (be) friendly to my family in future.
column14: The secretary (want) to know if the new Head (make) any changes.
column15: The doctor can’t say now when the patient (recover).
column16: Tell me if you (finish) your book in summer and when exactly you (do) it.
column17: People standing on the bus stop (wonder) if it (stop) raining soon.
column19: I wonder if he (be) late again.
column20: My dressmaker (have) no idea when my wedding dress (be) ready.
column21: Why are you measuring the cooker? – I’d like to see if it (fit) in my new kitchen.
column22: Tell me the exact time when they (come).
